---
title: "BrainAlign Runbook"
output: github_document
---

# Purpose

This runbook explains the order to run the consolidated scripts on the training PC.

All user-facing shell / PowerShell / Python entry points now live in `scripts/`.
The notebooks remain for exploratory analysis only.

The project now uses:

- one shared CLIP space across EEG, MEG, and fMRI
- modality manifests for each native image set
- pairwise and 3-way intersection manifests for conversion

# One-Time Setup

From the repo root:

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
python -m venv .venv
.\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

The training scripts will auto-detect `.venv` first, then `venv`, then fall back to `python`.

# Optional Sanity Checks

These are lightweight and safe to run before training:

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/test_model.py
./.venv/bin/python scripts/print_shapes.py
./.venv/bin/python scripts/print_fmri_struct.py
./.venv/bin/python scripts/test_meg_metadata.py
./.venv/bin/python scripts/test_pd.py
```

Windows PowerShell equivalents:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/test_model.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/print_shapes.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/print_fmri_struct.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/test_meg_metadata.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/test_pd.py
```

# Required Data Contracts

Before correct MEG training can start, the repo needs a THINGS image-number map at `data/things_image_map.tsv` or another path set in `config.yaml` under `data.things_image_map_path`.

Required columns:

- `image_number`
- `image_id`

Recommended:

- `relative_path`

If that file is missing but the repo contains `osfstorage-archive/01_image-level/image-paths.csv`, `scripts/build_image_manifests.py` will auto-generate `data/things_image_map.tsv`.

If neither is present, `scripts/build_image_manifests.py` will write `data/manifests/things_image_map.template.tsv`. Populate it from your full THINGS metadata and rerun the manifest build step.

If you want a universal CLIP cache across all modalities, point `data.things_image_root` in `config.yaml` at the full THINGS image root, or pass `--image-root` explicitly to `scripts/build_clip_cache.py`.

# Recommended Run Order On The PC

## 1. Build modality manifests

Run this first to see whether the THINGS image map is present and to generate the modality manifests.

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_image_manifests.py
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/build_image_manifests.py
```

Expected outputs:

- `data/manifests/eeg_all.tsv`
- `data/manifests/fmri_all.tsv`
- `data/manifests/meg_numeric.tsv`

If the THINGS image map is present, you will also get:

- `data/manifests/meg_all.tsv`
- `data/manifests/all_modalities_union.tsv`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_meg.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri_meg.txt`

If the map is missing, stop here, populate `data/things_image_map.tsv`, and rerun step 1.

## 2. Refresh the legacy shared-image file if you still need it

This is optional and mainly exists for compatibility with older workflows.

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_shared_images.py
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/build_shared_images.py
```

If `data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt` exists, this keeps `data/shared_images.txt` in sync with it.

## 3. Build the universal CLIP cache

This is the key step that lets EEG, MEG, and fMRI all target the same CLIP space.

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_clip_cache.py \
  --manifest data/manifests/all_modalities_union.tsv \
  --image-root data/things-images
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts/build_clip_cache.py `
  --manifest data/manifests/all_modalities_union.tsv `
  --image-root data/things-images
```

If your full THINGS images live somewhere else, either pass that path to `--image-root` or update `data.things_image_root` in `config.yaml`.

## 4. Train or resume full-data retrieval models

These runs are for modality-to-image and image-to-modality retrieval.

### MEG full-data retrieval

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --resume
```

### fMRI full-data retrieval

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --resume
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -Resume
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -Resume
```

The MEG checkpoints now use the `_attnpool` stem.

## 5. Evaluate full-data retrieval models

Run this after the full-data checkpoints exist.

### MEG retrieval

Example for subject 1:

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality meg \
  --subject 1 \
  --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_attnpool_best.pt \
  --split test
```

### fMRI retrieval

Example for subject 3:

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality fmri \
  --subject 3 \
  --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_best.pt \
  --split test
```

Outputs are written under `results/meg/` and `results/fmri/`.

Windows PowerShell uses the same arguments with `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe`.

## 6. Train or resume shared-only conversion models

These runs are only for cross-modality conversion on the shared image intersection.

Use explicit manifests for conversion experiments so the overlap is unambiguous. Examples:

- `data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_meg.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri_meg.txt`

### MEG shared-only conversion model

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --resume
```

### fMRI shared-only conversion model

```bash
./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --resume
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -SharedOnly -SharedManifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt -Resume
.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -SharedOnly -SharedManifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt -Resume
```

Shared-only checkpoints are saved with `_shared` in the filename so they do not overwrite the full-data retrieval checkpoints.

## 7. Evaluate shared-only retrieval models

This is optional but useful to confirm the shared-subset models are healthy before conversion.

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality meg \
  --subject 1 \
  --shared-only \
  --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt \
  --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_attnpool_shared_best.pt \
  --split test
```

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py \
  --modality fmri \
  --subject 3 \
  --shared-only \
  --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt \
  --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_shared_best.pt \
  --split test
```

## 8. Run modality-conversion evaluation

Once both shared-only checkpoints exist:

```bash
./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_conversion.py \
  --source-modality meg \
  --source-subject 1 \
  --source-ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub01_attnpool_shared_best.pt \
  --target-modality fmri \
  --target-subject 3 \
  --target-ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub03_shared_best.pt \
  --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt \
  --split test
```

This writes results under `results/conversion/`.

For EEG conversion experiments, use the same command pattern with:

- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_meg.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri.txt`
- `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri_meg.txt`

# Suggested PC Execution Order

Run these in this order:

1. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/test_model.py`
2. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/print_shapes.py`
3. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_image_manifests.py`
4. Populate `data/things_image_map.tsv` if step 3 writes the template instead of the mapped manifests
5. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_image_manifests.py` again after the map is in place
6. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/build_clip_cache.py --manifest data/manifests/all_modalities_union.tsv --image-root data/things-images`
7. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --resume`
8. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --resume`
9. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality meg --subject <id> --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<id>_attnpool_best.pt --split test`
10. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality fmri --subject <id> --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<id>_best.pt --split test`
11. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality meg --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --resume`
12. `./scripts/train_all_subjects.sh --modality fmri --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --resume`
13. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality meg --subject <id> --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<id>_attnpool_shared_best.pt --split test`
14. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_retrieval.py --modality fmri --subject <id> --shared-only --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<id>_shared_best.pt --split test`
15. `./.venv/bin/python scripts/evaluate_conversion.py --source-modality meg --source-subject <meg_id> --source-ckpt checkpoints/meg/meg_brainalign_sub<meg_id>_attnpool_shared_best.pt --target-modality fmri --target-subject <fmri_id> --target-ckpt checkpoints/fmri/fmri_brainalign_sub<fmri_id>_shared_best.pt --shared-manifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt --split test`

If the PC is running Windows PowerShell, replace steps 7, 8, 11, and 12 with:

1. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -Resume`
2. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -Resume`
3. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality meg -SharedOnly -SharedManifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt -Resume`
4. `.\scripts\train_all_subjects.ps1 -Modality fmri -SharedOnly -SharedManifest data/manifests/intersections/fmri_meg.txt -Resume`

# Notes

- EEG checkpoints are already present and can still be evaluated separately.
- Full-data retrieval and shared-only conversion are intentionally separate workflows.
- Pairwise conversion should use the corresponding pairwise manifest.
- 3-way conversion should use `data/manifests/intersections/eeg_fmri_meg.txt`.
- The conversion evaluator aggregates embeddings per `image_id` after aligning the requested shared manifest.
